title: Convert to smaller units (g and kg)
description: Practice converting a metric measure of mass to a smaller unit.
topic_path: Module 2: Unit conversions and problem solving with metric measurement
Topic B: Application of metric unit conversions
Convert to smaller units (g and kg)

hints: We need to convert $\blueD5$ kilograms $\maroonD{45}$ grams to grams.

First, let's convert $\blueD5$ kilograms to grams.

$1 \text{ kilogram} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ grams}$


$\blueD{5} \text{ kilograms} = \blueD{5}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{5{,}000}\text{ grams}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{45}$ grams.

$\blueD{5{,}000}\text{ grams}+\maroonD{45}\text{ grams}=\blueD{5}{,}\blueD{0}\maroonD{45}\text{ grams}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeter| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{14}$ kilograms $\maroonD{300}$ grams to grams.

First, let's convert $\blueD{14}$ kilograms to grams.

$1 \text{ kilogram} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ grams}$


$\blueD{14} \text{ kilograms} = \blueD{14}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{14{,}000}\text{ grams}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{300}$ grams.

$\blueD{14{,}000}\text{ grams}+\maroonD{300}\text{ grams}=\blueD{14}{,}\maroonD{300}\text{ grams}$

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{7}$ kilograms $\maroonD{8}$ grams to grams.

First, let's convert $\blueD{7}$ kilograms to grams.

$1 \text{ kilogram} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ grams}$


$\blueD{7} \text{ kilograms} = \blueD{7}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{7{,}000}\text{ grams}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{8}$ grams.

$\blueD{7{,}000}\text{ grams}+\maroonD{8}\text{ grams}=\blueD{7}{,}\blueD{00}\maroonD{8}\text{ grams}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare using $>,<,$ or $=$.**

$7$  kilograms [[☃ dropdown 1]] $60{,}000$ grams 

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
To compare, we need the numbers to have the same units.  

Let's convert $\blueD7$ kilograms to grams.

Now, we can compare.

$\blueD{7{,}000} $ grams $< {60{,}000}$ grams
